$comment: Project-shared Claude Code permissions. Auto-approves the dev-loop scripts so
engineers don't get prompted on every iteration. Anything not on the allow
list still goes through the normal approval flow.
Categories:
1. Local dev-loop scripts under scripts/ (PowerShell + Bash equivalents)
2. Common Node/Prisma/npm commands needed during the inner-loop (db push,
seed, test, build) — these never touch the demo DB; DATABASE_URL is
scoped per command
3. Read-only docker queries against the local stack
4. Read-only gh CLI for monitoring CI runs
5. Wildcard PowerShell — Windows is the user's primary shell. Process
management (Get-NetTCPConnection / Stop-Process / Get-Process), file
queries, and ad-hoc one-liners come up too often to enumerate.
Destructive git/gh operations (push --force, gh pr merge, etc.) and
SSH to demo are NOT covered by this — those still need confirmation.
6. Skill-bundled scripts under .claude/skills/* — wire-in.sh, log.sh,
and any future helper bundled with a skill. Future skill authors
don't need to expand this list per-skill; the glob covers them.
7. Authoring + editing skills under .claude/skills/** — Write + Edit
are auto-approved so the agent doesn't get prompted on every new
SKILL.md / template / helper script. Skills live in-tree so they
ship with the repo; this is not config that should require
human-in-the-loop on each edit.
Personal overrides go in .claude/settings.local.json (gitignored).
